name: 50/30/20 Budget Guideline
description: A common heuristic where roughly 50% of take-home pay goes to needs, 30% to wants, and 20% to savings or debt repayment.
reference: Popular personal finance heuristic (e.g. Elizabeth Warren's rule of thumb).

name: Savings Rate Benchmarks
description: Saving at least 10% of income is a common starting target; 15–20% or more is often considered strong for long-term goals.
reference: General savings benchmarks from personal finance education resources.

name: Emergency Fund Guideline
description: Building an emergency fund that can cover 3–6 months of essential expenses can provide a buffer against unexpected events.

name: Overspending Detection
description: When total expenses regularly exceed monthly income, it indicates overspending that may require reducing expenses or increasing income.
reference: Basic budgeting principle: expenses should not consistently exceed income.

name: Housing Cost Guideline
description: Many budgeting guidelines suggest keeping housing costs near or below about 30% of gross income, though this can vary by location and situation.

name: Debt-to-Income Consideration
description: If a large portion of income goes toward debt payments, it can limit flexibility and increase financial stress; gradually reducing high-interest debt can help.
reference: General debt-to-income considerations in personal finance education.
